perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop + bbox prefilter (#215)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop

Replace the single IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector::Perform call (33.5%
of total instructions in the B4c callgrind profile, 5,591M instructions)
with a custom per-face loop over IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

Two sources of overhead are eliminated:

1. NCollection_Sequence heap management (~31% of total): the ShapeIntersector
   accumulates results in a heap-allocated NCollection_Sequence (ClearSeq,
   Find, AppendPoint, malloc/free).  The per-face loop instead tracks the
   closest hit in a stack-allocated scalar.

2. All-face intersection without prefiltering (~2%): per-face intersectors
   are gated behind Bnd_Box::IsOut(gp_Lin), rejecting faces whose AABB the
   ray misses in O(1).

Implementation details:

- IntersectorCache now holds std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IntCurvesFace_Intersector>>
  (one per face, heap-allocated to avoid movability requirements) and
  std::vector<Bnd_Box> expandedBoxes (each face box enlarged by
  IntersectionTolerance() to prevent IsOut false positives for zero-thickness
  planar face AABBs).

- GetOrCreateIntersector() rebuilds both vectors on generation mismatch.

- DistanceToOut no longer needs the fFaceAdaptorIndex hash-map lookup:
  the loop tracks minFaceIdx directly, giving O(1) access to the cached
  BRepAdaptor_Surface for normal computation.

- DistanceToOut normal condition updated to accept TopAbs_IN || TopAbs_ON,
  fixing pre-existing failures for sphere/cylinder seam hit points that
  IntCurvesFace_Intersector correctly classifies as ON (not IN).

Smart mouse mode: defer disable until click above viewport (#328)

In smart mode, mouse capture was disabled whenever the mouse moved above
the viewport — too aggressive, preventing hover/movement above the
prompt without losing capture. The intended trigger is user intent to
interact with terminal content above (e.g. text selection), which only a
click signals.

## Changes

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: In the smart mode `on_mouse` handler, gate the
`disable()` call on `MouseEventKind::Down(_)`. Events above the viewport
still return `false` (skipped by flyline) but no longer tear down
capture on mere movement.

```rust
if mouse.row < self.last_viewport_top {
    // Only disable on an actual click above the viewport
    if matches!(mouse.kind, MouseEventKind::Down(_)) {
        self.mouse_state.disable("smart mode: click above the viewport");
    }
    self.last_mouse_over_cell = None;
    return false;
}
```

- **`src/settings.rs`** / **`README.md`**: Updated `MouseMode::Smart`
doc and both README occurrences — "mouse moves above the viewport" →
"user clicks above the viewport".

The `update-settings-docs.yml` workflow only updated the main `flyline
--help` block in the README; the `<!--
FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END -->` block was left stale.

## Changes

- **`docker/flyline_help.Dockerfile`**: Added a `RUN` step to capture
`flyline create-anim --help` (ANSI-stripped) into
`/flyline_create_anim_help.txt`; exports it from the `scratch` stage
alongside `flyline_help.txt`.

- **`.github/workflows/update-settings-docs.yml`**: Extended the Python
README-update script to also read `flyline_create_anim_help.txt` and
replace the `FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END` markers:

```python
create_anim_help_text = pathlib.Path("flyline_create_anim_help.txt").read_text().rstrip("\n")
new_create_anim_block = f"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->\n```\n{create_anim_help_text}\n```\n<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->"
updated = re.sub(
    r"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->.*?<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->",
    new_create_anim_block,
    updated,
    flags=re.DOTALL,
)
```

G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: eliminate O(log T + k) sqrt calls per BVH safety query (#209)

* Initial plan

* Eliminate per-node/per-triangle sqrt calls in BVH traversal

Work in squared-distance space throughout PointToMeshDistance traversal
and take a single std::sqrt() in BVHLowerBoundDistance() after traversal
completes. This avoids O(log T + k) sqrt calls per safety query."

commit.message:
fix(blink): suppress completions in comments via sources.default instead of enabled()

Using enabled()=false to block comments also disables ALL blink keymaps,
causing Tab to fall through to consume_fallback_keys which inserts a
whitespace character.

Fix: move the comment detection into sources.default (a function called
per completion request). This keeps keymaps fully active while returning
an empty source list when cursor is inside a treesitter comment capture.

Also fold per_filetype (lua/css/scss/gitcommit) into the same function
so lazydev and sass-variables are also suppressed in comments — they
would otherwise bypass the check via inherit_defaults.

commit.message:
fix(blink): remove comment detection from enabled()

The comment detection was never part of the original config - it was
introduced during this session. It caused enabled() to return false
whenever '--' appeared before the cursor, which blocked blink's Tab
keymap (consume_fallback_keys ran instead), inserting whitespace.

Root cause: the check fired correctly for all states where '--' was
present before the cursor, including the auto-continued comment line
after pressing 'o', even if the user had only partially deleted '--'
when pressing Tab.

The fix is simply to remove the comment detection. Blink completions
in comments are arguably useful anyway.

commit.message:
fix(blink): rewrite Tab chain as explicit single function

The multi-command array chain (select_next -> snippet_forward -> function)
was unreliable because each step returns false independently, making it
hard to reason about when neotab runs. Rewrote as a single function with
explicit priority:

  1. Menu visible  -> select_next() — if it returns false (no items),
     the function also returns false; Tab is still consumed by the keymap
     callback (non-expr), so no space is inserted
  2. Snippet active -> snippet_forward()
  3. Otherwise     -> neotab tabout (bracket escape or indent via act_as_tab)

This guarantees neotab never runs while the menu is visible, regardless
of whether select_next succeeds or fails.

Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

fix(blink): guard comment detection against stale treesitter state

After pressing o under a comment Neovim auto-inserts -- via formatoptions.
If the user deletes -- and starts typing, treesitter has not yet re-parsed,
so get_captures_at_pos still returns a comment capture. The enabled()
function returned false, blink fell back to Neovim default Tab behaviour
(indent spaces), making the menu non-interactive.

leader (e.g. --) is actually present before the cursor. If it is not,
the line cannot be a comment regardless of what the stale parser reports.

Add explicit permissions to GitHub Actions workflows

to follow least-privilege principle for GITHUB_TOKEN.

  alive_drones and attacker_actions in digital_twin.py,

  in evaluation, training, tests, and visualization modules

commit.message:
fix: address code review feedback

- ForwardRenderer: use mat3 uniform for normal matrix (not mat4) and
  compute it via Mat3.normalMatrix() — avoids redundant 4x4 ops
- Camera: add public setOrbit(theta, phi, radius) method to avoid
  callers mutating private fields
- OpenHuman.load(): use camera.setOrbit() instead of private field access
- OpenHuman SDK camera namespace: use module-level scratch Vec3 for lookAt
  to eliminate per-call Float32Array allocation; expose setOrbit()
- demo/index.html: replace direct _camera private field access with
  instance.camera.setOrbit() public API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement pure WebGL 2.0 digital human render engine

Add complete engine implementation with zero runtime dependencies:

Core:
- GLContext: WebGL2 context with extension discovery and WebGL1 fallback
- StateCache: centralized GL state tracker to avoid redundant driver calls
- Buffer: VertexBuffer, IndexBuffer, UniformBuffer wrappers
- RenderTarget: MRT framebuffer with RGBA8/16F/32F color attachments
- Shader: compile/link, uniform location cache, typed setters

Math (Float32Array-backed, out-parameter style, no heap allocs in loops):
- Vec2, Vec3, Vec4 with full static ops
- Mat3: identity, multiply, transpose, invert, fromMat4, normalMatrix
- Mat4: identity, multiply, invert, perspective, orthographic, lookAt,
         translate, rotateX/Y/Z, fromQuat, fromRotationTranslationScale
- Quat: identity, multiply, normalize, slerp, fromEuler, fromAxisAngle,
        fromMat3, toMat4, conjugate, invert
- DualQuat: fromRotationTranslation, multiply, normalize, toMat4, lerp (ScLERP)

Renderer:
- ForwardRenderer: Phong/PBR-lite GLSL 300 es forward pass (functional)
- DeferredRenderer: scaffold with TODO comments
- PostProcessStack: scaffold with TODO comments
- ShadowMap: scaffold with TODO comments

Asset:
- GLTFLoader: glTF 2.0 + GLB loader, MRT vertex buffers, VAO creation,
  embedded/external buffers, PBR material parsing, Draco/basisu warnings

Scene:
- Node: TRS scene graph with recursive world matrix update
- Camera: perspective + orbit controls (mouse/touch drag + scroll/pinch zoom)
- Light: directional/point/ambient with cached output arrays
- Character: mesh container with animation/morph stubs

SDK:
- OpenHuman.load(url, canvas): factory returning OpenHumanInstance
- Procedural sphere demo mesh when url is null or '__demo__'
- animation, morph, streaming, camera, renderer namespaces
- RAF render loop with automatic canvas resize handling

Demo:
- demo/index.html: self-contained ES module demo, procedural sphere,
  orbit controls, FPS counter, auto-rotate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

development-release-update (#110)

* feat(styles): add animations, utilities, and inject barrel file

- _animations.scss: 12 keyframes + utility classes + reduced-motion support
- _utilities.scss: text-gradient, hover-lift, glass, badge, sr-only, skeleton
- _inject.scss: barrel file forwarding variables + mixins for Vite injection
- vite.config.js: use _inject.scss instead of separate imports

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(auth): UX improvements across auth and contact views

- SignupView: add reCAPTCHA, pass captchaToken to signup, add autocomplete attrs
- SigninView: add reCAPTCHA, forgot password link, redirect query passthrough, autocomplete attrs
- VerifyCodeView: use redirect query param, add back-to-login button
- ContactView: add reCAPTCHA, pass captchaToken in request body
- ForgotPasswordView: add autocomplete=email
- ResetPasswordView: add autocomplete attrs (email, one-time-code, new-password)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(layout): add mobile responsive header and structured footer

TheHeader:
- Add mobile navigation drawer with hamburger menu (d-md-none)
- Hide desktop nav on small screens (d-none d-md-flex)
- Mobile drawer includes nav links, language switcher, auth links
- Remove scoped styles to support navigation drawer styling

TheFooter:
- Restructure into responsive multi-column layout (v-row/v-col)
- Column 1: Brand/logo + description
- Column 2: Links (Home, Contact, Dashboard)
- Column 3: Legal (Privacy, Terms)
- Bottom bar: copyright with dynamic year + app version
- Full width on mobile, multi-column on desktop

i18n:
- Add nav.language key (EN/FR)
- Add footer.description, footer.links, footer.legal keys
- Update footer.copyright to use {year} parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [development-release-update]
